steps.title: Convert to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
From $ax + by = b$, solve for $y$:
$$by = -ax + b$$
$$y = -\frac{a}{b}x + 1$$
This shows the line has y-intercept $(0, 1)$ and slope $-\frac{a}{b}$.

steps.title: Analyze the slope given 0 < a < b
steps.content:
Since $a < b$, the fraction $\frac{a}{b} < 1$. Combined with $a > 0$ and $b > 0$, we have $0 < \frac{a}{b} < 1$. Therefore:
$$-1 < -\frac{a}{b} < 0$$
The slope is negative, but its magnitude is less than 1, creating a gentle negative slope.

steps.title: Find the x-intercept and identify the graph
steps.content:
Set $y = 0$ in $ax + by = b$:
$$ax = b$$
$$x = \frac{b}{a}$$
Since $b > a > 0$, we have $\frac{b}{a} > 1$. The line crosses the x-axis to the right of $x = 1$, passes through $(0, 1)$, and has a gentle negative slope. That matches choice C — C is correct.
